revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 神大根王 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[弥生時代]]、[[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 大根王、神骨、八瓜入日子王、八瓜命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = [[宗慶大塚古墳]]説あり | 官位 = 初代[[本巣国造]]（[[三野前国造]]・美濃国造） | 主君 = [[開化天皇]] | 氏族 = [[美濃氏|美濃直]]祖 | 父母 = 父：[[日子坐王]]、母：息長水依比売 | 兄弟 = 水穂真若王、水穂五百依比売、御井津比売 | 妻 = | 子 = 兄比売（兄遠子）、弟比売（弟遠子） | 特記事項 = }} '''神大根王'''（かむおおねのみこ、[[生没年不詳]]）は、[[古墳時代]]の人物で初代'''[[本巣国造]]'''（'''[[三野前国造]]'''・美濃国造）。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』では別名を'''八瓜入日子王'''（やつりのいりひこのみこ）と伝え、[[開化天皇]]記では[[本巣国造|三野国之本巣国造]]・長幡部連の祖、[[景行天皇]]記では'''大根王'''が[[美濃国造]]の祖としている。『[[日本書紀]]』でも『古事記』とほぼ同様に記され、景行天皇紀に'''神骨'''（かむほね）が美濃国造とされる。一方、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」では'''八瓜命'''を[[開化天皇|開化朝]]に[[三野前国造]]に定めたとされ、これら三書の記述から三野国之本巣国造と三野前国造は同一の国造とされる<ref>「古代中世 第一章 大化前代の加茂地方 第三節 国造と県主」『美濃加茂市史 通史編』[[美濃加茂市]]、1980年、202頁。</ref><ref>「第六章 大化前代 第二節 国造と県主」『各務原市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世』各務原市教育委員会、1986年、181-184頁。</ref><ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「三野前国造などの分岐と活動」『古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族』青垣出版、2021年、81、82頁。</ref>。なお、神大根王の名は「鴨の大根」を意味するものと見る説がある<ref name="kamo">宝賀寿男「三野前国造などの分岐と活動」『古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族』青垣出版、2021年、86、87頁。</ref>（[[#系譜|後述]]）。
岐阜県[[本巣市]]宗慶にある[[宗慶大塚古墳]]は神大根王またはその子孫の墓といわれ、その築造時期は[[古墳時代]]前期（[[4世紀]]）にまで遡る。このため、古くから王塚古墳と称されてきた。
[[高坂神社]]や[[伊波乃西神社]]で祀られる他、複数の神社の創祀伝承に登場する。[[加毛神社]]では、鴨君彦坐王の子・神大根王が美濃国造となって子孫が西南濃地方に繁行したため、開拓者たちは鴨氏の祖神・[[賀茂別雷命|神別雷命]]を奉斎したとされる<ref>「[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 加毛神社]」岐阜県神社庁公式ホームページ。</ref>。
== 系譜 == 父は[[開化天皇]]皇子の[[日子坐王]]、母は[[国忍富命]]の娘の[[息長水依比売]]とされ、同母兄妹として[[安国造]]の祖・水穂真若王や水穂五百依比売、御井津比売がいるとされる。また、兄比売と弟比売の二女がいるとされる。しかし、「国造本紀」の記事では開化朝に国造へ定められたとされ、『古事記』の系譜と時代が矛盾する。
実際の出自について、[[本巣国造]]・[[三野前国造]]の領域に鴨氏祖・[[八咫烏]]に通じる香良洲神社や加茂神社（[[輪之内町]]、[[北方町]]など）が存在すること、同族の長幡部連が一説に[[天羽槌雄神|天羽雷雄命]]の後裔とされること<ref>村雲清光所蔵『麻殖氏系譜并家領記』、東京大学史料編纂所データベース。</ref>、三野前国造一族から[[加茂郡]]を本拠とした鴨県主や畿内の鴨県主、鴨君を輩出したと見られること、[[加茂神社]]において神大根王の祖神を[[賀茂別雷命|神別雷命]]とすることなどから、神大根王を日子坐王の子とする記述は系譜仮冒であり、[[山城国]]の[[賀茂県主|鴨県主]]から分かれたものであるとの推定がある。この推定では、本巣国造・三野前国造が[[皇別]]氏族を称した理由について、神大根王が日子坐王の近親女性を妻に迎えたことにより、系譜を附合させた可能性を指摘している<ref name="kamo"/>。その場合、実際の父については鴨県主の祖・水奴伎命で、佐々乃彦命と兄弟であると推定されている<ref>宝賀寿男「資料編 第1図 鴨氏族の遠祖系図（推定試案）」『古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族』青垣出版、2021年、313頁。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] *[[国造]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:かむおおねのみこ}} [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:美濃国の人物]] [[Category:岐阜県の歴史]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

contributor.username: ja>学者気取りの先頭はここで終わり！

revision.comment: 戻し：「国造主」という学術用語なし。勝手な造語につき。

revision.text:
'''Kamioone no Mikoto''' (神大根王, dates of birth and death unknown) was an early figure of the [[Kofun period]] and the first '''[[Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko]]''' (本巣国造, also known as [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] or Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko).
== Overview == In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', he is also referred to as '''Yatsuri no Irihiko no Mikoto''' (八瓜入日子王). In the ''[[Kōka Tennōki]]'' section, he is identified as the ancestor of the [[Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko|Mino Province Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko]] and the Nagahata-no-Be lineage. In the ''[[Keikō Tennōki]]'', the name '''Ōone no Ō''' (大根王) is listed as the ancestor of the [[Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. Similarly, the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' contains parallel accounts, where the name '''Kamuhone''' (神骨) appears as the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko in the ''Keikō Tennōki''. Additionally, the ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' section states that '''Yatsuri no Mikoto''' was appointed as the [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] during the reign of [[Kōka Tennō|Emperor Kōka]]. Based on these records, the Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko and Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko are considered the same position.<ref>「古代中世 第一章 大化前代の加茂地方 第三節 国造と県主」『美濃加茂市史 通史編』[[美濃加茂市]]、1980年、202頁。</ref><ref>「第六章 大化前代 第二節 国造と県主」『各務原市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世』各務原市教育委員会、1986年、181-184頁。</ref><ref>{{Cite book|author=[[Toshio Hōga|宝賀寿男]]|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=81–82}}</ref>. The name ''Kamioone'' has been interpreted as meaning "the great root of Kamo."<ref name="kamo">{{Cite book|author=宝賀寿男|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=86–87}}</ref>
The {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|宗慶大塚古墳}}, located in {{ill|Motozu, Gifu Prefecture|ja|本巣市}}'s Sōkei district, is believed to be the burial site of Kamioone no Mikoto or his descendants. The tomb dates back to the early Kofun period (4th century) and has historically been referred to as ''Ōtsuka Kofun''.
He is enshrined at multiple shrines, including {{ill|Takasaka Shrine|ja|高坂神社}} and {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社}}, and appears in the founding traditions of several others. At {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|加毛神社}}, Kamioone no Mikoto is said to be the son of {{ill|Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|ja|鴨君彦坐王}} and the progenitor of the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko, with his descendants spreading throughout southwestern Mino. It is said that the settlers worshiped {{ill|Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命}}, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan.<ref>[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 加毛神社] (Kamo Shrine), official website of the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office.</ref>
== Genealogy == His father is said to be {{ill|Hikozaka no Ō|ja|日子坐王}}, a prince of [[Kōka Tennō]], and his mother is {{ill|Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|ja|息長水依比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Amenokage no Mikoto|ja|天御影命}}. He is believed to have had siblings, including {{ill|Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|ja|水穂真若王}}, the ancestor of the [[Ankoku no Miyatsuko]], and {{ill|Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|ja|水穂五百依比売}}, as well as {{ill|Mii no Tsuhime|ja|御井津比売}}. However, the chronology described in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' conflicts with the genealogical records in the ''Kojiki.''
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Topics == * [[List of figures before the Asuka period]] * [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:Kamioone no Mikoto}} [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Kofun period figures]] [[Category:People from Mino Province]] [[Category:History of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[ja:神大根王]]

'''Kamioone no Mikoto''' (神大根王, dates of birth and death unknown) was an early figure of the [[Kofun period]] and the first '''[[Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko]]''' (本巣国造, also known as [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] or Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko).
== Overview == In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', he is also referred to as '''Yatsuri no Irihiko no Mikoto''' (八瓜入日子王). In the ''[[Kōka Tennōki]]'' section, he is identified as the ancestor of the [[Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko|Mino Province Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko]] and the Nagahata-no-Be lineage. In the ''[[Keikō Tennōki]]'', the name '''Ōone no Ō''' (大根王) is listed as the ancestor of the [[Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. Similarly, the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' contains parallel accounts, where the name '''Kamuhone''' (神骨) appears as the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko in the ''Keikō Tennōki''. Additionally, the ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' section states that '''Yatsuri no Mikoto''' was appointed as the [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] during the reign of [[Kōka Tennō|Emperor Kōka]]. Based on these records, the Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko and Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko are considered the same position.<ref>「古代中世 第一章 大化前代の加茂地方 第三節 国造と県主」『美濃加茂市史 通史編』[[美濃加茂市]]、1980年、202頁。</ref><ref>「第六章 大化前代 第二節 国造と県主」『各務原市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世』各務原市教育委員会、1986年、181-184頁。</ref><ref>{{Cite book|author=[[Toshio Hōga|宝賀寿男]]|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=81–82}}</ref>. The name ''Kamioone'' has been interpreted as meaning "the great root of Kamo."<ref name="kamo">{{Cite book|author=宝賀寿男|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=86–87}}</ref>
The {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|宗慶大塚古墳}}, located in {{ill|Motozu, Gifu Prefecture|ja|本巣市}}'s Sōkei district, is believed to be the burial site of Kamioone no Mikoto or his descendants. The tomb dates back to the early Kofun period (4th century) and has historically been referred to as ''Ōtsuka Kofun''.
He is enshrined at multiple shrines, including {{ill|Takasaka Shrine|ja|高坂神社}} and {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社}}, and appears in the founding traditions of several others. At {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|加毛神社}}, Kamioone no Mikoto is said to be the son of {{ill|Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|ja|鴨君彦坐王}} and the progenitor of the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko, with his descendants spreading throughout southwestern Mino. It is said that the settlers worshiped {{ill|Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命}}, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan.<ref>[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 加毛神社] (Kamo Shrine), official website of the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office.</ref>
== Genealogy == His father is said to be {{ill|Hikozaka no Ō|ja|日子坐王}}, a prince of [[Kōka Tennō]], and his mother is {{ill|Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|ja|息長水依比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Amenokage no Mikoto|ja|天御影命}}. He is believed to have had siblings, including {{ill|Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|ja|水穂真若王}}, the ancestor of the [[Ankoku no Miyatsuko]], and {{ill|Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|ja|水穂五百依比売}}, as well as {{ill|Mii no Tsuhime|ja|御井津比売}}. However, the chronology described in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' conflicts with the genealogical records in the ''Kojiki.''
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Kamioone no Mikoto}} [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Kofun period figures]] [[Category:People from Mino Province]] [[Category:History of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[category:most recent 500]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Ōne no Mikoto]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:神大根王]]

'''Kamioone no Mikoto''' (神大根王, dates of birth and death unknown) was an early figure of the [[Kofun period]] and the first '''[[Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko]]''' (本巣国造, also known as [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] or Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko).
== Overview == In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', he is also referred to as '''Yatsuri no Irihiko no Mikoto''' (八瓜入日子王). In the ''[[Kōka Tennōki]]'' section, he is identified as the ancestor of the [[Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko|Mino Province Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko]] and the Nagahata-no-Be lineage. In the ''[[Keikō Tennōki]]'', the name '''Ōone no Ō''' (大根王) is listed as the ancestor of the [[Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. Similarly, the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' contains parallel accounts, where the name '''Kamuhone''' (神骨) appears as the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko in the ''Keikō Tennōki''. Additionally, the ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' section states that '''Yatsuri no Mikoto''' was appointed as the [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] during the reign of [[Kōka Tennō|Emperor Kōka]]. Based on these records, the Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko and Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko are considered the same position.<ref>「古代中世 第一章 大化前代の加茂地方 第三節 国造と県主」『美濃加茂市史 通史編』[[美濃加茂市]]、1980年、202頁。</ref><ref>「第六章 大化前代 第二節 国造と県主」『各務原市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世』各務原市教育委員会、1986年、181-184頁。</ref><ref>{{Cite book|author=[[Toshio Hōga|宝賀寿男]]|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=81–82}}</ref>. The name ''Kamioone'' has been interpreted as meaning "the great root of Kamo."<ref name="kamo">{{Cite book|author=宝賀寿男|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=86–87}}</ref>
The {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|宗慶大塚古墳}}, located in {{ill|Motozu, Gifu Prefecture|ja|本巣市}}'s Sōkei district, is believed to be the burial site of Kamioone no Mikoto or his descendants. The tomb dates back to the early Kofun period (4th century) and has historically been referred to as ''Ōtsuka Kofun''.
He is enshrined at multiple shrines, including {{ill|Takasaka Shrine|ja|高坂神社}} and {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社}}, and appears in the founding traditions of several others. At {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|加毛神社}}, Kamioone no Mikoto is said to be the son of {{ill|Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|ja|鴨君彦坐王}} and the progenitor of the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko, with his descendants spreading throughout southwestern Mino. It is said that the settlers worshiped {{ill|Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命}}, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan.<ref>[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 加毛神社] (Kamo Shrine), official website of the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office.</ref>
== Genealogy == His father is said to be {{ill|Hikozaka no Ō|ja|日子坐王}}, a prince of [[Kōka Tennō]], and his mother is {{ill|Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|ja|息長水依比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Amenokage no Mikoto|ja|天御影命}}. He is believed to have had siblings, including {{ill|Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|ja|水穂真若王}}, the ancestor of the [[Ankoku no Miyatsuko]], and {{ill|Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|ja|水穂五百依比売}}, as well as {{ill|Mii no Tsuhime|ja|御井津比売}}. However, the chronology described in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' conflicts with the genealogical records in the ''Kojiki.''
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

== Overview == In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', he is also referred to as '''Yatsuri no Irihiko no Mikoto''' (八瓜入日子王). In the ''[[Kōka Tennōki]]'' section, he is identified as the ancestor of the [[Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko|Mino Province Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko]] and the Nagahata-no-Be lineage. In the ''[[Keikō Tennōki]]'', the name '''Ōone no Ō''' (大根王) is listed as the ancestor of the [[Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. Similarly, the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' contains parallel accounts, where the name '''Kamuhone''' (神骨) appears as the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko in the ''Keikō Tennōki''. Additionally, the ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' section states that '''Yatsuri no Mikoto''' was appointed as the [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] during the reign of [[Kōka Tennō|Emperor Kōka]]. Based on these records, the Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko and Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko are considered the same position.<ref>「古代中世 第一章 大化前代の加茂地方 第三節 国造と県主」『美濃加茂市史 通史編』[[美濃加茂市]]、1980年、202頁。</ref><ref>「第六章 大化前代 第二節 国造と県主」『各務原市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世』各務原市教育委員会、1986年、181-184頁。</ref><ref>{{Cite book|author=[[Toshio Hōga|宝賀寿男]]|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=81–82}}</ref>. The name ''Kamioone'' has been interpreted as meaning "the great root of Kamo."<ref name="kamo">{{Cite book|author=宝賀寿男|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=86–87}}</ref>
The {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|宗慶大塚古墳}}, located in {{ill|Motozu, Gifu Prefecture|ja|本巣市}}'s Sōkei district, is believed to be the burial site of Kamioone no Mikoto or his descendants. The tomb dates back to the early Kofun period (4th century) and has historically been referred to as ''Ōtsuka Kofun''.
He is enshrined at multiple shrines, including {{ill|Takasaka Shrine|ja|高坂神社}} and {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社}}, and appears in the founding traditions of several others. At {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|加毛神社}}, Kamioone no Mikoto is said to be the son of {{ill|Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|ja|鴨君彦坐王}} and the progenitor of the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko, with his descendants spreading throughout southwestern Mino. It is said that the settlers worshiped {{ill|Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命}}, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan.<ref>[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 加毛神社] (Kamo Shrine), official website of the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office.</ref>
== Genealogy == His father is said to be {{ill|Hikozaka no Ō|ja|日子坐王}}, a prince of [[Kōka Tennō]], and his mother is {{ill|Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|ja|息長水依比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Amenokage no Mikoto|ja|天御影命}}. He is believed to have had siblings, including {{ill|Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|ja|水穂真若王}}, the ancestor of the [[Ankoku no Miyatsuko]], and {{ill|Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|ja|水穂五百依比売}}, as well as {{ill|Mii no Tsuhime|ja|御井津比売}}. However, the chronology described in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' conflicts with the genealogical records in the ''Kojiki.''

'''Kamioone no Mikoto''' (神大根王, dates of birth and death unknown) was an early figure of the [[Kofun period]] and the first '''[[Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko]]''' (本巣国造, also known as [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] or Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko).
== Overview == In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', he is also referred to as '''Yatsuri no Irihiko no Mikoto''' (八瓜入日子王). In the ''[[Kōka Tennōki]]'' section, he is identified as the ancestor of the [[Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko|Mino Province Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko]] and the Nagahata-no-Be lineage. In the ''[[Keikō Tennōki]]'', the name '''Ōone no Ō''' (大根王) is listed as the ancestor of the [[Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. Similarly, the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' contains parallel accounts, where the name '''Kamuhone''' (神骨) appears as the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko in the ''Keikō Tennōki''. Additionally, the ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' section states that '''Yatsuri no Mikoto''' was appointed as the [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] during the reign of [[Kōka Tennō|Emperor Kōka]]. Based on these records, the Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko and Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko are considered the same position.<ref>「古代中世 第一章 大化前代の加茂地方 第三節 国造と県主」『美濃加茂市史 通史編』[[美濃加茂市]]、1980年、202頁。</ref><ref>「第六章 大化前代 第二節 国造と県主」『各務原市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世』各務原市教育委員会、1986年、181-184頁。</ref><ref>{{Cite book|author=[[Toshio Hōga|宝賀寿男]]|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=81–82}}</ref>. The name ''Kamioone'' has been interpreted as meaning "the great root of Kamo."<ref name="kamo">{{Cite book|author=宝賀寿男|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=86–87}}</ref>
The {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|宗慶大塚古墳}}, located in {{ill|Motozu, Gifu Prefecture|ja|本巣市}}'s Sōkei district, is believed to be the burial site of Kamioone no Mikoto or his descendants. The tomb dates back to the early Kofun period (4th century) and has historically been referred to as ''Ōtsuka Kofun''.
He is enshrined at multiple shrines, including {{ill|Takasaka Shrine|ja|高坂神社}} and {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社}}, and appears in the founding traditions of several others. At {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|加毛神社}}, Kamioone no Mikoto is said to be the son of {{ill|Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|ja|鴨君彦坐王}} and the progenitor of the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko, with his descendants spreading throughout southwestern Mino. It is said that the settlers worshiped {{ill|Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命}}, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan.<ref>[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 加毛神社] (Kamo Shrine), official website of the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office.</ref>
== Genealogy == His father is said to be {{ill|Hikozaka no Ō|ja|日子坐王}}, a prince of [[Kōka Tennō]], and his mother is {{ill|Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|ja|息長水依比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Amenokage no Mikoto|ja|天御影命}}. He is believed to have had siblings, including {{ill|Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|ja|水穂真若王}}, the ancestor of the [[Ankoku no Miyatsuko]], and {{ill|Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|ja|水穂五百依比売}}, as well as {{ill|Mii no Tsuhime|ja|御井津比売}}. However, the chronology described in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' conflicts with the genealogical records in the ''Kojiki.''

However, the chronology described in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' conflicts with the genealogical records in the ''Kojiki.''
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
'''Kamioone no Mikoto''' (神大根王, dates of birth and death unknown) was an early figure of the [[Kofun period]] and the first '''[[Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko]]''' (本巣国造, also known as [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] or Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko).
== Overview == In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', he is also referred to as '''Yatsuri no Irihiko no Mikoto''' (八瓜入日子王). In the ''[[Kōka Tennōki]]'' section, he is identified as the ancestor of the [[Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko|Mino Province Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko]] and the Nagahata-no-Be lineage. In the ''[[Keikō Tennōki]]'', the name '''Ōone no Ō''' (大根王) is listed as the ancestor of the [[Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. Similarly, the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' contains parallel accounts, where the name '''Kamuhone''' (神骨) appears as the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko in the ''Keikō Tennōki''. Additionally, the ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' section states that '''Yatsuri no Mikoto''' was appointed as the [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] during the reign of [[Kōka Tennō|Emperor Kōka]]. Based on these records, the Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko and Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko are considered the same position.<ref>「古代中世 第一章 大化前代の加茂地方 第三節 国造と県主」『美濃加茂市史 通史編』[[美濃加茂市]]、1980年、202頁。</ref><ref>「第六章 大化前代 第二節 国造と県主」『各務原市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世』各務原市教育委員会、1986年、181-184頁。</ref><ref>{{Cite book|author=[[Toshio Hōga|宝賀寿男]]|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=81–82}}</ref>. The name ''Kamioone'' has been interpreted as meaning "the great root of Kamo."<ref name="kamo">{{Cite book|author=宝賀寿男|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=86–87}}</ref>
The {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|宗慶大塚古墳}}, located in {{ill|Motozu, Gifu Prefecture|ja|本巣市}}'s Sōkei district, is believed to be the burial site of Kamioone no Mikoto or his descendants. The tomb dates back to the early Kofun period (4th century) and has historically been referred to as ''Ōtsuka Kofun''.
He is enshrined at multiple shrines, including {{ill|Takasaka Shrine|ja|高坂神社}} and {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社}}, and appears in the founding traditions of several others. At {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|加毛神社}}, Kamioone no Mikoto is said to be the son of {{ill|Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|ja|鴨君彦坐王}} and the progenitor of the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko, with his descendants spreading throughout southwestern Mino. It is said that the settlers worshiped {{ill|Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命}}, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan.<ref>[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 加毛神社] (Kamo Shrine), official website of the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office.</ref>
== Genealogy == His father is said to be {{ill|Hikozaka no Ō|ja|日子坐王}}, a prince of [[Kōka Tennō]], and his mother is {{ill|Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|ja|息長水依比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Amenokage no Mikoto|ja|天御影命}}. He is believed to have had siblings, including {{ill|Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|ja|水穂真若王}}, the ancestor of the [[Ankoku no Miyatsuko]], and {{ill|Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|ja|水穂五百依比売}}, as well as {{ill|Mii no Tsuhime|ja|御井津比売}}. However, the chronology described in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' conflicts with the genealogical records in the ''Kojiki.''
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of figures before the Asuka period]] * [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]]

It is said that the settlers worshiped {{ill|Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命}}, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan.<ref>[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 加毛神社] (Kamo Shrine), official website of the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office.</ref>

He is believed to have had siblings, including {{ill|Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|ja|水穂真若王}}, the ancestor of the [[Ankoku no Miyatsuko]], and {{ill|Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|ja|水穂五百依比売}}, as well as {{ill|Mii no Tsuhime|ja|御井津比売}}. It is said that the settlers worshiped {{ill|Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命}}, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan.<ref>[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 加毛神社] (Kamo Shrine), official website of the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office.</ref>

In the ''[[Kōka Tennōki]]'' section, he is identified as the ancestor of the [[Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko|Mino Province Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko]] and the Nagahata-no-Be lineage. In the ''[[Keikō Tennōki]]'', the name '''Ōone no Ō''' (大根王) is listed as the ancestor of the [[Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. Similarly, the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' contains parallel accounts, where the name '''Kamuhone''' (神骨) appears as the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko in the ''Keikō Tennōki''. He is enshrined at multiple shrines, including {{ill|Takasaka Shrine|ja|高坂神社}} and {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社}}, and appears in the founding traditions of several others. He is believed to have had siblings, including {{ill|Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|ja|水穂真若王}}, the ancestor of the [[Ankoku no Miyatsuko]], and {{ill|Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|ja|水穂五百依比売}}, as well as {{ill|Mii no Tsuhime|ja|御井津比売}}. However, the chronology described in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' conflicts with the genealogical records in the ''Kojiki.''

The {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|宗慶大塚古墳}}, located in {{ill|Motozu, Gifu Prefecture|ja|本巣市}}'s Sōkei district, is believed to be the burial site of Kamioone no Mikoto or his descendants. The tomb dates back to the early Kofun period (4th century) and has historically been referred to as ''Ōtsuka Kofun''.
He is enshrined at multiple shrines, including {{ill|Takasaka Shrine|ja|高坂神社}} and {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社}}, and appears in the founding traditions of several others. At {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|加毛神社}}, Kamioone no Mikoto is said to be the son of {{ill|Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|ja|鴨君彦坐王}} and the progenitor of the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko, with his descendants spreading throughout southwestern Mino. It is said that the settlers worshiped {{ill|Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命}}, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan.<ref>[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 加毛神社] (Kamo Shrine), official website of the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office.</ref>
== Genealogy == His father is said to be {{ill|Hikozaka no Ō|ja|日子坐王}}, a prince of [[Kōka Tennō]], and his mother is {{ill|Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|ja|息長水依比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Amenokage no Mikoto|ja|天御影命}}. He is believed to have had siblings, including {{ill|Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|ja|水穂真若王}}, the ancestor of the [[Ankoku no Miyatsuko]], and {{ill|Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|ja|水穂五百依比売}}, as well as {{ill|Mii no Tsuhime|ja|御井津比売}}. However, the chronology described in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' conflicts with the genealogical records in the ''Kojiki.''

'''Kamioone no Mikoto''' (神大根王, dates of birth and death unknown) was an early figure of the [[Kofun period]] and the first '''[[Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko]]''' (本巣国造, also known as [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] or Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko).
== Overview == In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', he is also referred to as '''Yatsuri no Irihiko no Mikoto''' (八瓜入日子王). In the ''[[Kōka Tennōki]]'' section, he is identified as the ancestor of the [[Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko|Mino Province Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko]] and the Nagahata-no-Be lineage. In the ''[[Keikō Tennōki]]'', the name '''Ōone no Ō''' (大根王) is listed as the ancestor of the [[Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. Similarly, the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' contains parallel accounts, where the name '''Kamuhone''' (神骨) appears as the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko in the ''Keikō Tennōki''. Additionally, the ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' section states that '''Yatsuri no Mikoto''' was appointed as the [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] during the reign of [[Kōka Tennō|Emperor Kōka]]. Based on these records, the Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko and Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko are considered the same position.<ref>「古代中世 第一章 大化前代の加茂地方 第三節 国造と県主」『美濃加茂市史 通史編』[[美濃加茂市]]、1980年、202頁。</ref><ref>「第六章 大化前代 第二節 国造と県主」『各務原市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世』各務原市教育委員会、1986年、181-184頁。</ref><ref>{{Cite book|author=[[Toshio Hōga|宝賀寿男]]|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=81–82}}</ref>. The name ''Kamioone'' has been interpreted as meaning "the great root of Kamo."<ref name="kamo">{{Cite book|author=宝賀寿男|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=86–87}}</ref>
The {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|宗慶大塚古墳}}, located in {{ill|Motozu, Gifu Prefecture|ja|本巣市}}'s Sōkei district, is believed to be the burial site of Kamioone no Mikoto or his descendants. The tomb dates back to the early Kofun period (4th century) and has historically been referred to as ''Ōtsuka Kofun''.
He is enshrined at multiple shrines, including {{ill|Takasaka Shrine|ja|高坂神社}} and {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwase Shrine}}, and appears in the founding traditions of several others. At {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|加毛神社}}, Kamioone no Mikoto is said to be the son of {{ill|Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|ja|鴨君彦坐王}} and the progenitor of the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko, with his descendants spreading throughout southwestern Mino. It is said that the settlers worshiped {{ill|Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命}}, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan.<ref>[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 加毛神社] (Kamo Shrine), official website of the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office.</ref>
== Genealogy == His father is said to be {{ill|Hikozaka no Ō|ja|日子坐王}}, a prince of [[Kōka Tennō]], and his mother is {{ill|Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|ja|息長水依比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Amenokage no Mikoto|ja|天御影命}}. He is believed to have had siblings, including {{ill|Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|ja|水穂真若王}}, the ancestor of the [[Ankoku no Miyatsuko]], and {{ill|Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|ja|水穂五百依比売}}, as well as {{ill|Mii no Tsuhime|ja|御井津比売}}. However, the chronology described in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' conflicts with the genealogical records in the ''Kojiki.''
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of figures before the Asuka period]] * [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
'''Kamioone no Mikoto''' (神大根王, dates of birth and death unknown) was an early figure of the [[Kofun period]] and the first '''[[Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko]]''' (本巣国造, also known as [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] or Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko).
== Overview == In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', he is also referred to as '''Yatsuri no Irihiko no Mikoto''' (八瓜入日子王). In the ''[[Kōka Tennōki]]'' section, he is identified as the ancestor of the [[Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko|Mino Province Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko]] and the Nagahata-no-Be lineage. In the ''[[Keikō Tennōki]]'', the name '''Ōone no Ō''' (大根王) is listed as the ancestor of the [[Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. Similarly, the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' contains parallel accounts, where the name '''Kamuhone''' (神骨) appears as the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko in the ''Keikō Tennōki''. Additionally, the ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' section states that '''Yatsuri no Mikoto''' was appointed as the [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] during the reign of [[Kōka Tennō|Emperor Kōka]]. Based on these records, the Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko and Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko are considered the same position.<ref>「古代中世 第一章 大化前代の加茂地方 第三節 国造と県主」『美濃加茂市史 通史編』[[美濃加茂市]]、1980年、202頁。</ref><ref>「第六章 大化前代 第二節 国造と県主」『各務原市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世』各務原市教育委員会、1986年、181-184頁。</ref><ref>{{Cite book|author=[[Toshio Hōga|宝賀寿男]]|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=81–82}}</ref>. The name ''Kamioone'' has been interpreted as meaning "the great root of Kamo."<ref name="kamo">{{Cite book|author=宝賀寿男|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=86–87}}</ref>
The {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|宗慶大塚古墳}}, located in {{ill|Motozu, Gifu Prefecture|ja|本巣市}}'s Sōkei district, is believed to be the burial site of Kamioone no Mikoto or his descendants. The tomb dates back to the early Kofun period (4th century) and has historically been referred to as ''Ōtsuka Kofun''.
He is enshrined at multiple shrines, including {{ill|Takasaka Shrine|ja|高坂神社}} and {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine}}, and appears in the founding traditions of several others. At {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|加毛神社}}, Kamioone no Mikoto is said to be the son of {{ill|Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|ja|鴨君彦坐王}} and the progenitor of the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko, with his descendants spreading throughout southwestern Mino. It is said that the settlers worshiped {{ill|Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命}}, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan.<ref>[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 加毛神社] (Kamo Shrine), official website of the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office.</ref>
== Genealogy == His father is said to be {{ill|Hikozaka no Ō|ja|日子坐王}}, a prince of [[Kōka Tennō]], and his mother is {{ill|Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|ja|息長水依比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Amenokage no Mikoto|ja|天御影命}}. He is believed to have had siblings, including {{ill|Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|ja|水穂真若王}}, the ancestor of the [[Ankoku no Miyatsuko]], and {{ill|Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|ja|水穂五百依比売}}, as well as {{ill|Mii no Tsuhime|ja|御井津比売}}. However, the chronology described in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' conflicts with the genealogical records in the ''Kojiki.''
== Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also! == * [[List of figures before the Asuka period]] * [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:Kami Ōne}}{{citation needed|Kamioone no Mikoto}} [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Kofun period figures]] [[Category:People from Mino Province]] [[Category:History of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōne no Mikoto]][[Category:Ōne no Mikoto]][[Category:Ōne no Mikoto]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:神大根王]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kami Ōne]][[Category:11]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kami Ōne}}{{citation needed|Kami Ōne}}

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 神大根王 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[弥生時代]]、[[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 大根王、神骨、八瓜入日子王、八瓜命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = [[宗慶大塚古墳]]説あり | 官位 = 初代[[本巣国造]]（[[三野前国造]]・美濃国造） | 主君 = [[開化天皇]] | 氏族 = [[美濃氏|美濃直]]祖 | 父母 = 父：[[日子坐王]]、母：息長水依比売 | 兄弟 = 水穂真若王、水穂五百依比売、御井津比売 | 妻 = | 子 = 兄比売（兄遠子）、弟比売（弟遠子） | 特記事項 = }} '''神大根王'''（かむおおねのみこ、[[生没年不詳]]）は、[[古墳時代]]の人物で初代'''[[本巣国造]]'''（'''[[三野前国造]]'''・美濃国造）。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』では別名を'''八瓜入日子王'''（やつりのいりひこのみこ）と伝え、[[開化天皇]]記では[[本巣国造|三野国之本巣国造]]・長幡部連の祖、[[景行天皇]]記では'''大根王'''が[[美濃国造]]の祖としている。『[[日本書紀]]』でも『古事記』とほぼ同様に記され、景行天皇紀に'''神骨'''（かむほね）が美濃国造とされる。一方、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」では'''八瓜命'''を[[開化天皇|開化朝]]に[[三野前国造]]に定めたとされ、これら三書の記述から三野国之本巣国造と三野前国造は同一の国造とされる<ref>「古代中世 第一章 大化前代の加茂地方 第三節 国造と県主」『美濃加茂市史 通史編』[[美濃加茂市]]、1980年、202頁。</ref><ref>「第六章 大化前代 第二節 国造と県主」『各務原市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世』各務原市教育委員会、1986年、181-184頁。</ref><ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「三野前国造などの分岐と活動」『古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族』青垣出版、2021年、81、82頁。</ref>。なお、神大根王の名は「鴨の大根」を意味するものと見る説がある<ref name="kamo">宝賀寿男「三野前国造などの分岐と活動」『古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族』青垣出版、2021年、86、87頁。</ref>（[[#系譜|後述]]）。
岐阜県[[本巣市]]宗慶にある[[宗慶大塚古墳]]は神大根王またはその子孫の墓といわれ、その築造時期は[[古墳時代]]前期（[[4世紀]]）にまで遡る。このため、古くから王塚古墳と称されてきた。
[[高坂神社]]や[[伊波乃西神社]]で祀られる他、複数の神社の創祀伝承に登場する。[[加毛神社]]では、鴨君彦坐王の子・神大根王が美濃国造となって子孫が西南濃地方に繁行したため、開拓者たちは鴨氏の祖神・[[賀茂別雷命|神別雷命]]を奉斎したとされる<ref>「[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 加毛神社]」岐阜県神社庁公式ホームページ。</ref>。
== 系譜 == 父は[[開化天皇]]皇子の[[日子坐王]]、母は[[天御影命]]の娘の[[息長水依比売]]とされ、同母兄妹として[[安国造]]の祖・水穂真若王や水穂五百依比売、御井津比売がいるとされる。しかし、「国造本紀」の記事では開化朝に国造へ定められたとされ、『古事記』の系譜と時代が矛盾する。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] *[[国造]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:かむおおねのみこ}} [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:美濃国の人物]] [[Category:岐阜県の歴史]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

revision.text:
'''Kamioone no Mikoto''' (神大根王, dates of birth and death unknown) was an early figure of the [[Kofun period]] and the first '''[[Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko]]''' (本巣国造, also known as [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] or Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko).
== Overview == In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', he is also referred to as '''Yatsuri no Irihiko no Mikoto''' (八瓜入日子王). In the ''[[Kōka Tennōki]]'' section, he is identified as the ancestor of the [[Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko|Mino Province Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko]] and the Nagahata-no-Be lineage. In the ''[[Keikō Tennōki]]'', the name '''Ōone no Ō''' (大根王) is listed as the ancestor of the [[Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. Similarly, the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' contains parallel accounts, where the name '''Kamuhone''' (神骨) appears as the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko in the ''Keikō Tennōki''. Additionally, the ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' section states that '''Yatsuri no Mikoto''' was appointed as the [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] during the reign of [[Kōka Tennō|Emperor Kōka]]. Based on these records, the Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko and Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko are considered the same position.<ref>「古代中世 第一章 大化前代の加茂地方 第三節 国造と県主」『美濃加茂市史 通史編』[[美濃加茂市]]、1980th year 、202頁。</ref><ref>「第六章 大化前代 第二節 国造と県主」『各務原市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世』各務原市教育委員会、1986th year 、181-184頁。</ref><ref>{{Cite book|author=[[Toshio Hōga|宝賀寿男]]|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=81–82}}</ref>. The name ''Kamioone'' has been interpreted as meaning "the great root of Kamo."<ref name="kamo">{{Cite book|author=宝賀寿男|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=86–87}}</ref>
The {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|宗慶大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, located in {{ill|Motozu, Gifu Prefecture|ja|本巣市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motozu, Gifu Prefecture|qq=}}'s Sōkei district, is believed to be the burial site of Kamioone no Mikoto or his descendants. The tomb dates back to the early Kofun period (4th century) and has historically been referred to as ''Ōtsuka Kofun''.
He is enshrined at multiple shrines, including {{ill|Takasaka Shrine|ja|高坂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaka Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}, and appears in the founding traditions of several others. At {{ill|Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo Shrine|ja|加毛神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine|qq=}}, Kamioone no Mikoto is said to be the son of {{ill|Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|ja|鴨君彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|qq=}} and the progenitor of the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko, with his descendants spreading throughout southwestern Mino. It is said that the settlers worshiped {{ill|Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=}}, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan.<ref>[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 加毛神社] (Kamo Shrine), official website of the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office.</ref>
== Genealogy == His father is said to be {{ill|Hikozaka no Ō|ja|日子坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozaka no Ō|qq=}}, a prince of [[Kōka Tennō]], and his mother is {{ill|Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|ja|息長水依比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Amenokage no Mikoto|ja|天御影命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokage no Mikoto|qq=}}. He is believed to have had siblings, including {{ill|Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|ja|水穂真若王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|qq=}}, the ancestor of the [[Ankoku no Miyatsuko]], and {{ill|Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|ja|水穂五百依比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|qq=}}, as well as {{ill|Mii no Tsuhime|ja|御井津比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii no Tsuhime|qq=}}. However, the chronology described in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' conflicts with the genealogical records in the ''Kojiki.''
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of figures before the Asuka period]] * [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]]

In the ''[[Keikō Tennōki]]'', the name '''Ōone no Ō''' (大根王) is listed as the ancestor of the [[Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. The {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|宗慶大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, located in {{ill|Motozu, Gifu Prefecture|ja|本巣市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motozu, Gifu Prefecture|qq=}}'s Sōkei district, is believed to be the burial site of Kamioone no Mikoto or his descendants. At {{ill|Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo Shrine|ja|加毛神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine|qq=}}, Kamioone no Mikoto is said to be the son of {{ill|Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|ja|鴨君彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|qq=}} and the progenitor of the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko, with his descendants spreading throughout southwestern Mino. revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
'''Kamioone no Mikoto''' (神大根王, dates of birth and death unknown) was an early figure of the [[Kofun period]] and the first '''[[Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko]]''' (本巣国造, also known as [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] or Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko).
== Overview == In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', he is also referred to as '''Yatsuri no Irihiko no Mikoto''' (八瓜入日子王). In the ''[[Kōka Tennōki]]'' section, he is identified as the ancestor of the [[Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko|Mino Province Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko]] and the Nagahata-no-Be lineage. In the ''[[Keikō Tennōki]]'', the name '''Ōone no Ō''' (大根王) is listed as the ancestor of the [[Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. Similarly, the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' contains parallel accounts, where the name '''Kamuhone''' (神骨) appears as the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko in the ''Keikō Tennōki''. Additionally, the ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' section states that '''Yatsuri no Mikoto''' was appointed as the [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] during the reign of [[Kōka Tennō|Emperor Kōka]]. Based on these records, the Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko and Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko are considered the same position.<ref>「古代中世 第一章 大化前代の加茂地方 第三節 国造と県主」『美濃加茂市史 通史編』[[美濃加茂市]]、1980th year 、202頁。</ref><ref>「第六章 大化前代 第二節 国造と県主」『各務原市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世』各務原市教育委員会、1986th year 、181-184頁。</ref><ref>{{Cite book|author=[[Toshio Hōga|宝賀寿男]]|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=81–82}}</ref>. The name ''Kamioone'' has been interpreted as meaning "the great root of Kamo."<ref name="kamo">{{Cite book|author=宝賀寿男|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=86–87}}</ref>
The {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|宗慶大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, located in [[:en:Motosu, Gifu|Motozu, Gifu Prefecture]]'s Sōkei district, is believed to be the burial site of Kamioone no Mikoto or his descendants. The tomb dates back to the early Kofun period (4th century) and has historically been referred to as ''Ōtsuka Kofun''.
He is enshrined at multiple shrines, including {{ill|Takasaka Shrine|ja|高坂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaka Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}, and appears in the founding traditions of several others. At {{ill|Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo Shrine|ja|加毛神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine|qq=}}, Kamioone no Mikoto is said to be the son of {{ill|Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|ja|鴨君彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|qq=}} and the progenitor of the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko, with his descendants spreading throughout southwestern Mino. It is said that the settlers worshiped {{ill|Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=}}, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan.<ref>[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 加毛神社] (Kamo Shrine), official website of the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office.</ref>
== Genealogy == His father is said to be {{ill|Hikozaka no Ō|ja|日子坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozaka no Ō|qq=}}, a prince of [[Kōka Tennō]], and his mother is {{ill|Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|ja|息長水依比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Amenokage no Mikoto|ja|天御影命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokage no Mikoto|qq=}}. He is believed to have had siblings, including {{ill|Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|ja|水穂真若王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|qq=}}, the ancestor of the [[Ankoku no Miyatsuko]], and {{ill|Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|ja|水穂五百依比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|qq=}}, as well as {{ill|Mii no Tsuhime|ja|御井津比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii no Tsuhime|qq=}}. However, the chronology described in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' conflicts with the genealogical records in the ''Kojiki.''
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of figures before the Asuka period]] * [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]]
{{translated page|ja|神大根王|version=99874835|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== Genealogy == His father is said to be {{ill|Hikozaka no Ō|ja|日子坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozaka no Ō|qq=}}, a prince of [[Kōka Tennō]], and his mother is {{ill|Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|ja|息長水依比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Amenokage no Mikoto|ja|天御影命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokage no Mikoto|qq=}}. He is believed to have had siblings, including {{ill|Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|ja|水穂真若王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|qq=}}, the ancestor of the [[Ankoku no Miyatsuko]], and {{ill|Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|ja|水穂五百依比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|qq=}}, as well as {{ill|Mii no Tsuhime|ja|御井津比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii no Tsuhime|qq=}}. However, the chronology described in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' conflicts with the genealogical records in the ''Kojiki.''

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:美濃国の人物
revision.text:
'''Kamioone no Mikoto''' (神大根王, dates of birth and death unknown) was an early figure of the [[Kofun period]] and the first '''[[Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko]]''' (本巣国造, also known as [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] or Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko).
== Overview == In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', he is also referred to as '''Yatsuri no Irihiko no Mikoto''' (八瓜入日子王). In the ''[[Kōka Tennōki]]'' section, he is identified as the ancestor of the [[Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko|Mino Province Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko]] and the Nagahata-no-Be lineage. In the ''[[Keikō Tennōki]]'', the name '''Ōone no Ō''' (大根王) is listed as the ancestor of the [[Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. Similarly, the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' contains parallel accounts, where the name '''Kamuhone''' (神骨) appears as the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko in the ''Keikō Tennōki''. Additionally, the ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' section states that '''Yatsuri no Mikoto''' was appointed as the [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] during the reign of [[Kōka Tennō|Emperor Kōka]]. Based on these records, the Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko and Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko are considered the same position.<ref>「古代中世 第一章 大化前代の加茂地方 第三節 国造と県主」『美濃加茂市史 通史編』[[美濃加茂市]]、1980th year 、202頁。</ref><ref>「第六章 大化前代 第二節 国造と県主」『各務原市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世』各務原市教育委員会、1986th year 、181-184頁。</ref><ref>{{Cite book|author=[[Toshio Hōga|宝賀寿男]]|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=81–82}}</ref>. The name ''Kamioone'' has been interpreted as meaning "the great root of Kamo."<ref name="kamo">{{Cite book|author=宝賀寿男|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=86–87}}</ref>
The {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|宗慶大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, located in [[:en:Motosu, Gifu|Motozu, Gifu Prefecture]]'s Sōkei district, is believed to be the burial site of Kamioone no Mikoto or his descendants. The tomb dates back to the early Kofun period (4th century) and has historically been referred to as ''Ōtsuka Kofun''.
He is enshrined at multiple shrines, including {{ill|Takasaka Shrine|ja|高坂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaka Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}, and appears in the founding traditions of several others. At {{ill|Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo Shrine|ja|加毛神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine|qq=}}, Kamioone no Mikoto is said to be the son of {{ill|Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|ja|鴨君彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|qq=}} and the progenitor of the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko, with his descendants spreading throughout southwestern Mino. It is said that the settlers worshiped {{ill|Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=}}, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan.<ref>[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 加毛神社] (Kamo Shrine), official website of the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office.</ref>
== Genealogy == His father is said to be {{ill|Hikozaka no Ō|ja|日子坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozaka no Ō|qq=}}, a prince of [[Kōka Tennō]], and his mother is {{ill|Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|ja|息長水依比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Amenokage no Mikoto|ja|天御影命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokage no Mikoto|qq=}}. He is believed to have had siblings, including {{ill|Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|ja|水穂真若王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|qq=}}, the ancestor of the [[Ankoku no Miyatsuko]], and {{ill|Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|ja|水穂五百依比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|qq=}}, as well as {{ill|Mii no Tsuhime|ja|御井津比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii no Tsuhime|qq=}}. However, the chronology described in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' conflicts with the genealogical records in the ''Kojiki.''
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of figures before the Asuka period]] * [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]]

Based on these records, the Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko and Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko are considered the same position.<ref>「古代中世 第一章 大化前代の加茂地方 第三節 国造と県主」『美濃加茂市史 通史編』[[美濃加茂市]]、1980th year 、202頁。</ref><ref>「第六章 大化前代 第二節 国造と県主」『各務原市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世』各務原市教育委員会、1986th year 、181-184頁。</ref><ref>{{Cite book|author=[[Toshio Hōga|宝賀寿男]]|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=81–82}}</ref>. The name ''Kamioone'' has been interpreted as meaning "the great root of Kamo."<ref name="kamo">{{Cite book|author=宝賀寿男|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=86–87}}</ref>
The {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|宗慶大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, located in [[:en:Motosu, Gifu|Motozu, Gifu Prefecture]]'s Sōkei district, is believed to be the burial site of Kamioone no Mikoto or his descendants. The tomb dates back to the early Kofun period (4th century) and has historically been referred to as ''Ōtsuka Kofun''.
He is enshrined at multiple shrines, including {{ill|Takasaka Shrine|ja|高坂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaka Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}, and appears in the founding traditions of several others. At {{ill|Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo Shrine|ja|加毛神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine|qq=}}, Kamioone no Mikoto is said to be the son of {{ill|Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|ja|鴨君彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the progenitor of the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko, with his descendants spreading throughout southwestern Mino. It is said that the settlers worshiped {{ill|Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan.<ref>[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 加毛神社] (Kamo Shrine), official website of the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office.</ref>
== Genealogy == His father is said to be {{ill|Hikozaka no Ō|ja|日子坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozaka no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦坐王}}, a prince of [[Kōka Tennō]], and his mother is {{ill|Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|ja|息長水依比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the daughter of {{ill|Amenokage no Mikoto|ja|天御影命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokage no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天目一箇神}}. He is believed to have had siblings, including {{ill|Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|ja|水穂真若王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestor of the [[Ankoku no Miyatsuko]], and {{ill|Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|ja|水穂五百依比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as {{ill|Mii no Tsuhime|ja|御井津比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii no Tsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. However, the chronology described in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' conflicts with the genealogical records in the ''Kojiki.''
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
'''Kamioone no Mikoto''' (神大根王, dates of birth and death unknown) was an early figure of the [[Kofun period]] and the first '''[[Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko]]''' (本巣国造, also known as [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] or Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko).
== Overview == In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', he is also referred to as '''Yatsuri no Irihiko no Mikoto''' (八瓜入日子王). In the ''[[Kōka Tennōki]]'' section, he is identified as the ancestor of the [[Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko|Mino Province Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko]] and the Nagahata-no-Be lineage. In the ''[[Keikō Tennōki]]'', the name '''Ōone no Ō''' (大根王) is listed as the ancestor of the [[Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. Similarly, the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' contains parallel accounts, where the name '''Kamuhone''' (神骨) appears as the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko in the ''Keikō Tennōki''. Additionally, the ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' section states that '''Yatsuri no Mikoto''' was appointed as the [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] during the reign of [[Kōka Tennō|Emperor Kōka]]. Based on these records, the Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko and Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko are considered the same position.<ref>「古代中世 第一章 大化前代の加茂地方 第三節 国造と県主」『美濃加茂市史 通史編』[[美濃加茂市]]、1980th year 、202頁。</ref><ref>「第六章 大化前代 第二節 国造と県主」『各務原市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世』各務原市教育委員会、1986th year 、181-184頁。</ref><ref>{{Cite book|author=[[Toshio Hōga|宝賀寿男]]|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=81–82}}</ref>. The name ''Kamioone'' has been interpreted as meaning "the great root of Kamo."<ref name="kamo">{{Cite book|author=宝賀寿男|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=86–87}}</ref>
The {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|宗慶大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, located in [[:en:Motosu, Gifu|Motozu, Gifu Prefecture]]'s Sōkei district, is believed to be the burial site of Kamioone no Mikoto or his descendants. The tomb dates back to the early Kofun period (4th century) and has historically been referred to as ''Ōtsuka Kofun''.
He is enshrined at multiple shrines, including {{ill|Takasaka Shrine|ja|高坂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaka Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}, and appears in the founding traditions of several others. At {{ill|Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo Shrine|ja|加毛神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine|qq=}}, Kamioone no Mikoto is said to be the son of {{ill|Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|ja|鴨君彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the progenitor of the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko, with his descendants spreading throughout southwestern Mino. It is said that the settlers worshiped {{ill|Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan.<ref>[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 加毛神社] (Kamo Shrine), official website of the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office.</ref>
== Genealogy == His father is said to be {{ill|Hikozaka no Ō|ja|日子坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozaka no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦坐王}}, a prince of [[Kōka Tennō]], and his mother is {{ill|Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|ja|息長水依比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the daughter of {{ill|Amenokage no Mikoto|ja|天御影命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokage no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天目一箇神}}. He is believed to have had siblings, including {{ill|Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|ja|水穂真若王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestor of the [[Ankoku no Miyatsuko]], and {{ill|Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|ja|水穂五百依比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as {{ill|Mii no Tsuhime|ja|御井津比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii no Tsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Genealogy == His father is said to be {{ill|Hikozaka no Ō|ja|日子坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozaka no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦坐王}}, a prince of [[Kōka Tennō]], and his mother is {{ill|Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|ja|息長水依比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the daughter of {{ill|Amenokage no Mikoto|ja|天御影命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokage no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天目一箇神}}. He is believed to have had siblings, including {{ill|Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|ja|水穂真若王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestor of the [[Ankoku no Miyatsuko]], and {{ill|Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|ja|水穂五百依比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as {{ill|Mii no Tsuhime|ja|御井津比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii no Tsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. However, the chronology described in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' conflicts with the genealogical records in the ''Kojiki.''
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of figures before the Asuka period]] * [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]]

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:生没年不詳]] into [[Category:Year of Birth and Death unknown]] via bot
revision.text:
'''Kamioone no Mikoto''' (神大根王, dates of birth and death unknown) was an early figure of the [[Kofun period]] and the first '''[[Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko]]''' (本巣国造, also known as [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] or Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko).
== Overview == In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', he is also referred to as '''Yatsuri no Irihiko no Mikoto''' (八瓜入日子王). In the ''[[Kōka Tennōki]]'' section, he is identified as the ancestor of the [[Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko|Mino Province Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko]] and the Nagahata-no-Be lineage. In the ''[[Keikō Tennōki]]'', the name '''Ōone no Ō''' (大根王) is listed as the ancestor of the [[Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. Similarly, the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' contains parallel accounts, where the name '''Kamuhone''' (神骨) appears as the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko in the ''Keikō Tennōki''. Additionally, the ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' section states that '''Yatsuri no Mikoto''' was appointed as the [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] during the reign of [[Kōka Tennō|Emperor Kōka]]. Based on these records, the Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko and Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko are considered the same position.<ref>「古代中世 第一章 大化前代の加茂地方 第三節 国造と県主」『美濃加茂市史 通史編』[[:en:美濃加茂市|美濃加茂市]]、1980th year 、202頁。</ref><ref>「第六章 大化前代 第二節 国造と県主」『各務原市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世』各務原市教育委員会、1986th year 、181-184頁。</ref><ref>{{Cite book|author=[[Toshio Hōga|宝賀寿男]]|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=81–82}}</ref>. The name ''Kamioone'' has been interpreted as meaning "the great root of Kamo."<ref name="kamo">{{Cite book|author=宝賀寿男|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=86–87}}</ref>
The {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|宗慶大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, located in [[:en:Motosu, Gifu|Motozu, Gifu Prefecture]]'s Sōkei district, is believed to be the burial site of Kamioone no Mikoto or his descendants. The tomb dates back to the early Kofun period (4th century) and has historically been referred to as ''Ōtsuka Kofun''.
He is enshrined at multiple shrines, including {{ill|Takasaka Shrine|ja|高坂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaka Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}, and appears in the founding traditions of several others. At {{ill|Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo Shrine|ja|加毛神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine|qq=}}, Kamioone no Mikoto is said to be the son of {{ill|Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|ja|鴨君彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the progenitor of the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko, with his descendants spreading throughout southwestern Mino. It is said that the settlers worshiped {{ill|Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan.<ref>[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 加毛神社] (Kamo Shrine), official website of the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office.</ref>
== Genealogy == His father is said to be {{ill|Hikozaka no Ō|ja|日子坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozaka no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦坐王}}, a prince of [[Kōka Tennō]], and his mother is {{ill|Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|ja|息長水依比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the daughter of {{ill|Amenokage no Mikoto|ja|天御影命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokage no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天目一箇神}}. He is believed to have had siblings, including {{ill|Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|ja|水穂真若王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestor of the [[Ankoku no Miyatsuko]], and {{ill|Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|ja|水穂五百依比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as {{ill|Mii no Tsuhime|ja|御井津比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii no Tsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. However, the chronology described in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' conflicts with the genealogical records in the ''Kojiki.''
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of figures before the Asuka period]] * [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]]

revision.comment: Bot: prepend Wikidata AFC comment via pages.txt
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q119380336]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q119380336]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamioone no Mikoto''' (神大根王, dates of birth and death unknown) was an early figure of the [[Kofun period]] and the first '''[[Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko]]''' (本巣国造, also known as [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] or Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko).
== Overview == In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', he is also referred to as '''Yatsuri no Irihiko no Mikoto''' (八瓜入日子王). In the ''[[Kōka Tennōki]]'' section, he is identified as the ancestor of the [[Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko|Mino Province Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko]] and the Nagahata-no-Be lineage. In the ''[[Keikō Tennōki]]'', the name '''Ōone no Ō''' (大根王) is listed as the ancestor of the [[Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. Similarly, the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' contains parallel accounts, where the name '''Kamuhone''' (神骨) appears as the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko in the ''Keikō Tennōki''. Additionally, the ''[[:en:Sendai Kuji Hongi|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' section states that '''Yatsuri no Mikoto''' was appointed as the [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] during the reign of [[Kōka Tennō|Emperor Kōka]]. Based on these records, the Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko and Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko are considered the same position.<ref>「古代中世 第一章 大化前代の加茂地方 第三節 国造と県主」『美濃加茂市史 通史編』[[:en:美濃加茂市|美濃加茂市]]、1980th year 、202頁。</ref><ref>「第六章 大化前代 第二節 国造と県主」『各務原市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世』各務原市教育委員会、1986th year 、181-184頁。</ref><ref>{{Cite book|author=[[Toshio Hōga|宝賀寿男]]|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=81–82}}</ref>. The name ''Kamioone'' has been interpreted as meaning "the great root of Kamo."<ref name="kamo">{{Cite book|author=宝賀寿男|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=86–87}}</ref>
The {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|宗慶大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, located in [[:en:Motosu, Gifu|Motozu, Gifu Prefecture]]'s Sōkei district, is believed to be the burial site of Kamioone no Mikoto or his descendants. The tomb dates back to the early Kofun period (4th century) and has historically been referred to as ''Ōtsuka Kofun''.
He is enshrined at multiple shrines, including {{ill|Takasaka Shrine|ja|高坂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaka Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}, and appears in the founding traditions of several others. At {{ill|Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo Shrine|ja|加毛神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine|qq=}}, Kamioone no Mikoto is said to be the son of {{ill|Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|ja|鴨君彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the progenitor of the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko, with his descendants spreading throughout southwestern Mino. It is said that the settlers worshiped {{ill|Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan.<ref>[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 加毛神社] (Kamo Shrine), official website of the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office.</ref>
== Genealogy == His father is said to be {{ill|Hikozaka no Ō|ja|日子坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozaka no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦坐王}}, a prince of [[Kōka Tennō]], and his mother is {{ill|Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|ja|息長水依比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the daughter of {{ill|Amenokage no Mikoto|ja|天御影命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokage no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天目一箇神}}. He is believed to have had siblings, including {{ill|Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|ja|水穂真若王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestor of the [[Ankoku no Miyatsuko]], and {{ill|Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|ja|水穂五百依比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as {{ill|Mii no Tsuhime|ja|御井津比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii no Tsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. However, the chronology described in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' conflicts with the genealogical records in the ''Kojiki.''
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of figures before the Asuka period]] * [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]]

The {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|宗慶大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, located in [[:en:Motosu, Gifu|Motozu, Gifu Prefecture]]'s Sōkei district, is believed to be the burial site of Kamioone no Mikoto or his descendants. At {{ill|Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo Shrine|ja|加毛神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine|qq=}}, Kamioone no Mikoto is said to be the son of {{ill|Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|ja|鴨君彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the progenitor of the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko, with his descendants spreading throughout southwestern Mino. It is said that the settlers worshiped {{ill|Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan.<ref>[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 加毛神社] (Kamo Shrine), official website of the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office.</ref>

He is believed to have had siblings, including {{ill|Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|ja|水穂真若王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestor of the [[Ankoku no Miyatsuko]], and {{ill|Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|ja|水穂五百依比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as {{ill|Mii no Tsuhime|ja|御井津比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii no Tsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Similarly, the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' contains parallel accounts, where the name '''Kamuhone''' (神骨) appears as the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko in the ''Keikō Tennōki''. Additionally, the ''[[:en:Sendai Kuji Hongi|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' section states that '''Yatsuri no Mikoto''' was appointed as the [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] during the reign of [[Kōka Tennō|Emperor Kōka]]. Based on these records, the Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko and Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko are considered the same position.<ref>「古代中世 第一章 大化前代の加茂地方 第三節 国造と県主」『美濃加茂市史 通史編』[[:en:美濃加茂市|美濃加茂市]]、1980th year 、202頁。</ref><ref>「第六章 大化前代 第二節 国造と県主」『各務原市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世』各務原市教育委員会、1986th year 、181-184頁。</ref><ref>{{Cite book|author=[[Toshio Hōga|宝賀寿男]]|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=81–82}}</ref>. The name ''Kamioone'' has been interpreted as meaning "the great root of Kamo."<ref name="kamo">{{Cite book|author=宝賀寿男|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=86–87}}</ref>
The {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|宗慶大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, located in [[:en:Motosu, Gifu|Motozu, Gifu Prefecture]]'s Sōkei district, is believed to be the burial site of Kamioone no Mikoto or his descendants. The tomb dates back to the early Kofun period (4th century) and has historically been referred to as ''Ōtsuka Kofun''.
He is enshrined at multiple shrines, including {{ill|Takasaka Shrine|ja|高坂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaka Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}, and appears in the founding traditions of several others. At {{ill|Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo Shrine|ja|加毛神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine|qq=}}, Kamioone no Mikoto is said to be the son of {{ill|Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|ja|鴨君彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the progenitor of the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko, with his descendants spreading throughout southwestern Mino. It is said that the settlers worshiped {{ill|Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}}, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan.<ref>[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 加毛神社] (Kamo Shrine), official website of the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office.</ref>
== Genealogy == His father is said to be {{ill|Hikozaka no Ō|ja|日子坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozaka no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦坐王|1=Hikoimasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487910}}, a prince of [[Kōka Tennō]], and his mother is {{ill|Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|ja|息長水依比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the daughter of {{ill|Amenokage no Mikoto|ja|天御影命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokage no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天目一箇神|1=Ame no Mahitotsu no kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10016750}}. He is believed to have had siblings, including {{ill|Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|ja|水穂真若王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestor of the [[Ankoku no Miyatsuko]], and {{ill|Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|ja|水穂五百依比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as {{ill|Mii no Tsuhime|ja|御井津比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii no Tsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. However, the chronology described in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' conflicts with the genealogical records in the ''Kojiki.''

The name ''Kamioone'' has been interpreted as meaning "the great root of Kamo."<ref name="kamo">{{Cite book|author=宝賀寿男|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=86–87}}</ref>
The {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|宗慶大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, located in [[:en:Motosu, Gifu|Motozu, Gifu Prefecture]]'s Sōkei district, is believed to be the burial site of Kamioone no Mikoto or his descendants. The tomb dates back to the early Kofun period (4th century) and has historically been referred to as ''Ōtsuka Kofun''.
He is enshrined at multiple shrines, including {{ill|Takasaka Shrine|ja|高坂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaka Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579610}} and {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}, and appears in the founding traditions of several others. At {{ill|Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo Shrine|ja|加毛神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine|qq=}}, Kamioone no Mikoto is said to be the son of {{ill|Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|ja|鴨君彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the progenitor of the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko, with his descendants spreading throughout southwestern Mino. It is said that the settlers worshiped {{ill|Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}}, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan.<ref>[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 加毛神社] (Kamo Shrine), official website of the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office.</ref>
== Genealogy == His father is said to be {{ill|Hikozaka no Ō|ja|日子坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozaka no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦坐王|1=Hikoimasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487910}}, a prince of [[Kōka Tennō]], and his mother is {{ill|Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|ja|息長水依比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the daughter of {{ill|Amenokage no Mikoto|ja|天御影命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokage no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天目一箇神|1=Ame no Mahitotsu no kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10016750}}. He is believed to have had siblings, including {{ill|Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|ja|水穂真若王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestor of the [[Ankoku no Miyatsuko]], and {{ill|Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|ja|水穂五百依比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578864}}, as well as {{ill|Mii no Tsuhime|ja|御井津比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii no Tsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578867}}. However, the chronology described in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' conflicts with the genealogical records in the ''Kojiki.''
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
