== Genealogy == His father is said to be {{ill|Hikozaka no Ō|ja|日子坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozaka no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦坐王|1=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487910}}, a prince of [[Kōka Tennō]], and his mother is {{ill|Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|ja|息長水依比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the daughter of {{ill|Amenokage no Mikoto|ja|天御影命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokage no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天目一箇神|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10016750}}. Similarly, the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' contains parallel accounts, where the name '''Kamuhone''' (神骨) appears as the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko in the ''Keikō Tennōki''. Additionally, the ''[[:en:Sendai Kuji Hongi|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' section states that '''Yatsuri no Mikoto''' was appointed as the [[Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko]] during the reign of [[Kōka Tennō|Emperor Kōka]]. Based on these records, the Motozu Kuni no Miyatsuko and Minomae Kuni no Miyatsuko are considered the same position.<ref>「古代中世 第一章 大化前代の加茂地方 第三節 国造と県主」『美濃加茂市史 通史編』[[:en:美濃加茂市|美濃加茂市]]、1980th year 、202頁。</ref><ref>「第六章 大化前代 第二節 国造と県主」『各務原市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世』各務原市教育委員会、1986th year 、181-184頁。</ref><ref>{{Cite book|author=[[Toshio Hōga|宝賀寿男]]|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=81–82}}</ref>. The name ''Kamioone'' has been interpreted as meaning "the great root of Kamo."<ref name="kamo">{{Cite book|author=宝賀寿男|title=古代氏族の研究⑱ 鴨氏・服部氏 少彦名神の後裔諸族|publisher=青垣出版|year=2021|pages=86–87}}</ref>
The {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|宗慶大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, located in {{ill|Motosu, Gifu|en|Motosu, Gifu|qid=Q844047|lt=Motozu, Gifu Prefecture|1=Motosu, Gifu}}'s Sōkei district, is believed to be the burial site of Kamioone no Mikoto or his descendants. The tomb dates back to the early Kofun period (4th century) and has historically been referred to as ''Ōtsuka Kofun''.
He is enshrined at multiple shrines, including {{ill|Takasaka Shrine|ja|高坂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579610|1=Takasaka Shrine}} and {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}, and appears in the founding traditions of several others. At {{ill|Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo Shrine|ja|加毛神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine|qq=}}, Kamioone no Mikoto is said to be the son of {{ill|Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|ja|鴨君彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Hikozaka no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the progenitor of the Mino Kuni no Miyatsuko, with his descendants spreading throughout southwestern Mino. It is said that the settlers worshiped {{ill|Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamowake Ikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11634943}}, the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan.<ref>[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=64&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%8A%A0%E6%AF%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 加毛神社] (Kamo Shrine), official website of the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office.</ref>
== Genealogy == His father is said to be {{ill|Hikozaka no Ō|ja|日子坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozaka no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦坐王|1=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11487910}}, a prince of [[Kōka Tennō]], and his mother is {{ill|Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|ja|息長水依比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu Nagamizu Yorihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the daughter of {{ill|Amenokage no Mikoto|ja|天御影命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokage no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天目一箇神|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10016750}}. He is believed to have had siblings, including {{ill|Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|ja|水穂真若王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuho no Mawaka no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestor of the [[Ankoku no Miyatsuko]], and {{ill|Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|ja|水穂五百依比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuho no Iyo Yorihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578864|1=Mizuho Ioyori-hime}}, as well as {{ill|Mii no Tsuhime|ja|御井津比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii no Tsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578867|1=Miitsu-hime}}. However, the chronology described in the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi'' conflicts with the genealogical records in the ''Kojiki.''
